reasoning:
The question asks if there will be at least 1 riot in Ireland (as defined by ACLED) in the 30 days prior to four resolution dates. ACLED's baseline of 0.667 riots per 30 days implies there were exactly 8 riots in Ireland in the preceding 360 days. Riots (like the July 2024 Coolock unrest or February 2025 incidents) tend to be clustered over consecutive days, meaning fewer than 8 out of the 12 months in the year actually experience a riot. I estimate the base rate of a random 30-day window having at least 1 riot to be roughly 35-40%. 

For the first date (April 20), the 30-day window spans March 21 to April 20. Extensive searches for any riots, unrest, or significant public order offenses in Ireland during the past 23 days (March 21 to April 13) yielded no results, strongly suggesting 0 riots have occurred so far in this window. With only 7 days remaining, the probability is quite low, mostly consisting of the small chance of an event in the next week or a minor incident that escaped national news but was picked up by ACLED. 

For the subsequent dates (May 13, July 12, Oct 10), the 30-day windows lie entirely in the future. I use the ~36% base rate for the May date. For dates further out in the horizon, I gradually increase the probability towards 0.5 to reflect growing uncertainty over social, political, or economic conditions that shape public demonstrations and potential riots.
